justification: All three ACs present with exact values ($47.80 -> 47, $0.99 -> 0, 12-month expiry). Both domain rules present (1 pt/dollar floor-rounded, 12-month inactivity expiry). Both architecture functions referenced (earnPoints, checkExpiry) with implementation notes including Math.floor and date comparison. The 365-day vs 12-month terminology discrepancy is explicitly flagged and resolved in contradictions_resolved. No invented information — open_questions are clearly marked as unresolved rather than assumed.

observations.text: AC2 ($0.99 -> 0 points) explicitly preserved as a testable assertion and a standalone acceptance criterion
365-day vs 12-month conflict identified, explained with leap-year reasoning, and resolved in favor of Bird's domain authority
loyalty.ts file location and pure function design clearly specified in architecture guidance
Open questions are genuine ambiguities from the source outputs, not invented context
Terminology alignment section maps domain terms to technical terms without losing 'no purchase in 12 months' wording
Minor: resolution example states 'cutoff is 2025-03-24, not 2025-03-24 minus 1 day' which is confusingly worded — both resolve to nearly the same date in a non-leap year, weakening the illustration
